title: Trebla
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{FAreview}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Trebla_portrait_1.png]] |name=Trebla |homeworld=[[Endor/Legends|Endor]]{{Ewoks|The Curse of the Jindas}} |birth= |death= |species=[[Jinda/Legends|Jinda]]{{Ewoks|The Travelling Jindas}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]{{Fact}} |pronouns=He/him{{Fact}} |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes=Black ([[Eye/Legends|pupils]]) |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Travelling Jindas]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Here's Trebla the magician!|Bondo|}} '''Trebla''', a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jinda/Legends|Jinda]], was a [[Magic/Legends|magician]] and a member of the [[Travelling Jindas]] [[Entertainer|troupe]] that wandered across the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon]] of [[Endor (planet)/Legends|Endor]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. Originally [[Farm/Legends|farmers]] who lived in the domain of the [[Moon/Legends|moon's]] [[Rock Wizard]], Trebla and his kin were [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|forced]] into a nomadic lifestyle after the Wizard lost a [[Tooth/Legends|tooth]] and blamed the Jindas for his consequent pain. They traveled across Endor and performed for various audiences, all the while on the run from the Wizard's [[Force power/Legends|curse]], which caused large rocks to attack them if they remained sedentary for too long a [[Time/Legends|time]]. Trebla wowed crowds by apparently [[Telekinesis/Legends|levitating]] his assistants, who were in fact pulled into the air by [[Rope/Legends|ropes]] and harnesses. Circa 3.5 ABY, Trebla and the rest of the Travelling Jindas performed for the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] of [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]], and a young Ewok stowaway named [[Latara/Legends|Latara]] afterward became the magician's assistant. Trebla helped rescue her when she was kidnapped by a [[Gorneesh's tribe|tribe]] of [[Dulok/Legends|Duloks]], and not long after she returned to her [[City/Legends|village]], the Rock Wizard's curse caught up to the Jindas when large floating rocks pursued them after they relaxed near a [[Water/Legends|waterfall]] for several [[Standard day/Legends|days]]. A chance encounter with Latara and another rescue, that time from a group of [[Skandit]]s, brought Trebla and his troupe back to Bright Tree Village, where the Rock Wizard confronted them and learned the true source of his pain. Realizing his mistake, he freed the Jindas from their curse. ==Biography== ===Newly nomadic=== {{Quote|The amazing Trebla!|Bondo, to the Ewoks of Bright Tree Village|}} [[File:TreblaMagic-TTJ.png|thumb|left|225px|Trebla demonstrates his "powers."]] Trebla was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jinda/Legends|Jinda]] who lived among a group of his [[Species/Legends|species]] on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon]] of [[Endor (planet)/Legends|Endor]] [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].{{CSWECite|II|164|Jinda}}''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' Their home was an [[Farm/Legends|agricultural]] settlement that fell in the domain of the [[Rock Wizard]], a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] being{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/feature20081201.html|text=Castaways of Endor|int=Castaways of Endor}} who protected the Jindas in exchange for food obtained from their farming. One [[Standard day/Legends|day]], Trebla and four other Jindas brought some [[Pie/Legends|pie]] to the Rock Wizard's [[Rock Fortress|fortress]]. After losing a [[Tooth/Legends|tooth]] during his meal, their protector inferred from the pain that the Jindas had attempted to [[Poison/Legends|poison]] him, and Trebla and the others fled the fortress as the pie was thrown back at them. The Rock Wizard subsequently [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|banished]] the Jindas from their home and [[Force power/Legends|cursed]] them: if they ever returned or remained in one place for too long, the Jindas would be attacked by large rocks. During a [[Water/Legends|rainfall]], Trebla and the others left their home on a caravan of [[awor]]s—beasts they had formerly used to plow fields—and, suddenly nomadic, set themselves up as the [[Travelling Jindas]]. Trebla became a [[Magic/Legends|magician]] among [[entertainer]]s who journeyed throughout Endor's [[Tree/Legends|forests]] and performed for audiences that they encountered, asking only for food and one night's lodging in return. In the latter half of [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'' the Travelling Jindas arrived at the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] settlement of [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]]. Trebla and some of his fellows dismounted from the caravan and watched as [[Bondo]], their [[Chief/Legends|chief]],{{CSWECite|I|85|Bondo}} convinced the leaders of the [[City/Legends|village]] to allow them to perform. The Jindas then set up a stage and put on a show; when Trebla's act came on, he waved his [[Limb/Legends|hands]] and made it appear as if his [[Trebla's assistant|assistant]]—who actually wore a harness attached to [[Rope/Legends|ropes]]—[[Telekinesis/Legends|levitated]] above the stage. The Ewok audience was captivated by his "powers." At the performance's end, the entire company returned to the stage as Bondo said goodnight. The Travelling Jindas [[Sleep/Legends|slept]] in tents around the village that night and set off the next morning with a stowaway: the young Ewok [[Latara/Legends|Latara]], who felt that her [[Flute/Legends|flute]]-playing abilities would be better suited to the Jindas' lifestyle. ===A troublesome assistant=== {{Quote|No music tonight. Tonight, you are… Trebla's assistant!|Bondo, to Latara|}} [[File:TheShowMustGoOn-TTJ.png|thumb|right|225px|Trebla and Bondo prepare to deceive the Duloks.]] That night, the group came across sleeping [[Choreamnos|tree goats]] and set up another show. Bondo recruited Latara to be Trebla's assistant, and the two Jindas fit her with a harness before taking the stage. When Bondo introduced Trebla, he waved his hands to make the young Ewok "levitate," but a [[Blasé tree|tree]] [[Goat/Legends|goat]] took a bite out of one of her ropes, forcing Latara to grab onto a nearby branch and lower herself back onto the stage. The lethargic audience failed to take notice of the entire performance, let alone the slip-up, and the dejected Jindas left the next morning without their hoped-for tips. Latara was kidnapped that day by [[Unidentified Dulok (O)|two]] [[Unidentified Dulok (X)|Duloks]], but four of her Ewok friends caught up to the Jindas and convinced them to perform for the [[Gorneesh's tribe|Dulok tribe]] that night in order to mount a rescue. They took their caravan to the [[Dulok Swamp]], where the [[Dulok/Legends|Dulok]] [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Urgah]] eagerly consented to a performance. When Trebla was brought on stage, Bondo called for an audience volunteer and immediately picked Latara, whom the Dulok [[King/Legends|King]] [[Gorneesh/Legends|Gorneesh]] was forcing to babysit his [[Unidentified Dulok cub 1|three]] [[Unidentified Dulok cub 2|unruly]] [[Unidentified Dulok cub 3|children]]. Bondo distracted the gathered Duloks as Trebla fit Latara with a harness, the ropes to be pulled by her friends, who were positioned above the stage. Trebla's attempts at faux-telekinesis were thwarted by a knot in one of the ropes, and as Latara played her flute to stall for [[Time/Legends|time]], the other Ewoks worked to untie the problem snag. The rowdy crowd responded by throwing [[Fruit/Legends|fruit]], which prompted Trebla and Bondo to vacate the stage; the other Ewoks in turn crashed onto the stage and were forced to flee from Gorneesh's [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]]. The Jindas quickly packed up and left themselves. After making camp in a nearby clearing, they were joined by the five Ewoks, who had managed to escape from their pursuers. Having had a change of [[Heart/Legends|heart]], Latara decided to return home with her friends. ===The curse catches up=== {{Quote|Quick. Tie these two up. I'll release the Ewoks.''"
"''You saved us!''"
"''Of course. You didn't think we could leave without our favorite Ewok and her friends, do you?|Trebla and Latara, while escaping from the Skandits|}} [[File:Trebla_portrait_2.png|thumb|left|200px|Trebla gasps at the sight of floating rocks.]] Not long afterward, the Travelling Jindas made camp by a waterfall for some relaxation. On three consecutive days, Trebla reminded Bondo of the Rock Wizard's curse and suggested that they move on, but the comfortable chief repeatedly shrugged off his concerns. After his third snub, a frustrated Trebla wandered into the forest, where he spied several large floating rocks. He shouted to his comrades that the curse was upon them, and the Jindas quickly packed up and left. They shortly came upon a group of [[Skandit]]s, and when they dismounted from the caravan to promote their show, Trebla and Bondo overheard a familiar flute melody. Latara and her friends were [[Prisoner/Legends|imprisoned]] by the Skandits in the trunk of a tree, and when Trebla and Bondo's attempts to communicate with the Ewoks were met with hostility by the Skandits, they walked away to plan both the show and a [[Prison/Legends|prison]] break. During the evening's performance, two [[Jinda Dancers|dancers]] approached the prison's two Skandit guards and incapacitated them by rubbing cloths in their faces. Trebla used the opportunity to release the Ewoks and provide them with a large stage costume as a disguise. He told them to make their way to waiting awors, but a side trip across the stage caused both the costume and the ruse to collapse. Ewoks and Jindas alike fled from the angry Skandits, and the awor caravan arrived at Bright Tree Village the next day. The Ewok Chief [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]] thanked the Jindas for bringing Latara and her friends safely home, but the mirth and merriment were interrupted by large approaching rocks. The Jindas were given refuge in the treetop village, and as the floating rocks maintained a groundside vigil, Bondo told the Ewoks the tale of the Rock Wizard's curse. [[Logray/Legends|Logray]], the village's [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]], invoked the [[The Force/Legends|power]] of the [[Light Spirit]] and called upon [[Plant/Legends|vines]] from the forest to wrap around the rocks and tie them in place. Trebla, Bondo, and several of the Ewoks then descended to the ground, but a celebration was forestalled by the appearance of a fissure in the forest floor, out of which rose the Rock Wizard. Trebla and Bondo cowered on the ground, and when the Wizard demanded to know who had countered his magic, the two Jindas immediately pointed out Logray. The Rock Wizard was unswayed by the shaman's attempts at reasoning, and he used his magic to create more fissures in the ground, one of which sprouted a tall tree that crashed through and destroyed part of the Ewok village. His [[Terrorist/Legends|terror]] ceased only when he spotted his missing tooth, worn on a string around the neck of the Ewok [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]]. Kintaka had believed it to be a "lucky stone," but she returned it to the Wizard, who placed it in his mouth and immediately realized that it had been the true source of his pain. Trebla and Bondo stood up from their cowering as the Rock Wizard happily declared that the Jindas could return to their home, and the magician joined in a round of good-natured laughter. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Bondo, we must move on. You know of the curse.''"
"''I know, I know. We'll leave… tomorrow.''"
"''That's what you said yesterday. And the day before. Just wait. When it happens, he'll say, 'Why didn't you tell me?'|Trebla and Bondo|}} [[File:Trebla-TCotJ.png|thumb|right|225px|Trebla mutters to himself regarding Bondo's indifference toward the curse.]] As the Travelling Jindas' magician, Trebla dazzled audiences with his skills in "levitation," which were in fact parlor tricks achieved by the use of ropes and harnesses. He wore magician's garb from head to toe that included a [[Color/Legends|purple]] hat and robes as well as blue [[Glove/Legends|gloves]]. Trebla sported the outfit at all times, even when the Jindas were not performing, and during the time that they worked as farmers. Only Trebla's black-pupiled [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and long brown [[Hair/Legends|hair]] were visible to others. The Rock Wizard's curse was a worry of Trebla's, and when his fellows chose to remain sedentary, indifference toward his lone voice of dissent caused him to frustratedly mutter to himself. He displayed no bravery when confronted with the curse, fleeing from attacking rocks, cowering in front of the Rock Wizard, and immediately fingering Logray as the person who had countered the Wizard's magic. However, Trebla helped Latara escape from both the Skandits and the Duloks. He took something of a shine to his temporary assistant, referring to her as the Jindas' favorite Ewok. ==Behind the scenes== Trebla appears in "[[The Travelling Jindas]]" and "[[The Curse of the Jindas]]," two episodes of the [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']] animated television series that first aired in [[October]] [[1985]]. Both episodes were written by [[Bob Carrau]] and directed by [[Raymond Jafelice]],{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}} and the former was [[Star Wars home video releases#Ewoks|released on DVD]] in [[2004]] as part of ''[[Tales from the Endor Woods]]'', a compilation of four ''Ewoks'' episodes. The DVD subtitles reveal the correct spelling of the character's name.''[[Tales from the Endor Woods]]'' Although he does not have any lines in "The Travelling Jindas," Trebla was voiced by [[Alan Fawcett]] in ''The Curse of the Jindas'',{{BookCite|author=Ericson, Hal|container=|title=Television Cartoon Shows: An Illustrated Encyclopedia, 1949–2003|version=2nd ed. Vol. I: The Shows A–L. 2 vols|number=|publisher=McFarland & Co., Inc., Publishers|date=2005|pages=306}} although uncredited. In both episodes, Trebla's appearance is disguised by his hat and robes, but this article assumes he is a Jinda due to his Jinda-like hair and numerous references to his troupe being composed of Jindas. Similarly, Trebla's sex is never explicitly revealed, but this article treats the character as a male due to the contrast between his masculine voice and the more feminine voices of several female Jindas. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Trebla}} *''[[Tales from the Endor Woods]]'' {{C|Compilation of "[[Wicket's Wagon]]," "[[The Travelling Jindas]]," "[[To Save Deej]]," and "[[Asha (episode)|Asha]]"}} *{{Ewoks|The Travelling Jindas}} {{1st}} *{{Ewoks|The Curse of the Jindas}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |nl=Trebla }} [[Category:Entertainers]] [[Category:Jindas]] [[Category:Males]]
